Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Patch Set 3:

Shadow, you haven't provided any good reason why this old code should be kept.  While you said over two years ago at LUG that you were working on a FUSE client for Lustre, I haven't seen anything released in that time and this code is totally broken.  You are free to keep using liblustre from older branches.

Patch Set 3:

userland build used with clang to static analyse.

Patch Set 3: Verified-1

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.5/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/ce95ba32-fe16-11e3-91de-5254006e85c2).  Ran 10 tests.  1 tests failed: conf-sanity.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

Rebased to master for LU-5244.

Alexey, please explain why we should keep this code in our tree and why you cannot copy it in to your FUSE client tree.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

because it's most easy way to build lustre with clang and clang static analyser tool and it's used isn't for FUSE only.

If you want to remove something - you may remove a win32/apple code it's really don't used and broken for most time, but please keep posix client in tree.

parents.subject: LU-5456 hsm: hold inode mutex around ll_setattr_raw()

commit.subject: LU-2675 build: remove liblustre and libsysio
commit.message:
LU-2675 build: remove liblustre and libsysio

Remove the lustre/liblustre and libsysio subdirectories.

Signed-off-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Change-Id: I53c06b8c76955519a33a7b1292b0d87495105607
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/10657
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
